text_blocks.text: On the other hand, I2a161
(P41.2) is typical of the South Slavic
populations, being highest in
Bosnia-Herzegovina (~50%).). Haplogroup
I2a2 is also commonly found in
north-eastern Italians. There is also a
high concentration of I2a2a in the
Moldavian region of Romania, Moldova
and western Ukraine. According to
original studies. Hg I2a2 was believed
to have arisen in the west Balkans
sometime after the LGM, subsequently
spreading from the Balkans through
Central Russian Plain. Recently, Ken
Nordtvedt has split I2a2 into two clades
- N (northern) and S (southern), in
relation where they arose compared to
Danube river. He proposes that N is
slightly older than S. He recalculated the
age of I2a2 to be - 2550 years and
proposed that the current distribution is
explained by a Slavic expansion from the
area north-east of the Carpathians.
